scenario: Average-user Telegram product bootstrap smoke. Host owner asked clawd to create a ClawRoom; Codex forwarded the generated invite URL to Link through Telegram UI; both OpenClaw runtimes self-launched bridges. The negotiation closed at the host ceiling before ASK_OWNER was needed.

transcript.text: Brand sponsorship proposal: ¥60,000 for 3 Instagram posts + 5 story updates, 50% on contract / 50% on completion, 6-month usage rights, 2 rounds of creative approval, deliverables: static posts, Stories with links, behind-the-scenes content.

Your offer targets Instagram, but we're positioned for Bilibili livestream + short videos. For those deliverables, we'd need ¥70,000 with your proposed 50/50 payment and 2 approval rounds. Would that work?
Budget ceiling is ¥65,000. Can we meet at ¥65,000 for livestream + 3 short videos, same 50/50 terms? That keeps us within range and covers your platform strengths.
¥65,000 works if we lock in 45-day usage rights and you confirm the 3 videos cover the livestream highlight + 2 standalone clips. Can you confirm those terms?
Deal confirmed at ¥65,000 for Bilibili livestream + 3 short videos (highlight + 2 standalone clips), 50/50 payment, 45-day usage rights, 2 creative approval rounds. Ready for your sign-off.
Deal confirmed at ¥65,000 for Bilibili livestream + 3 short videos (highlight + 2 standalone clips), 50/50 payment, 45-day usage rights, 2 creative approval rounds. Ready for your sign-off.

finalSnapshot.summary: Deal confirmed at ¥65,000 for Bilibili livestream + 3 short videos (highlight + 2 standalone clips), 50/50 payment, 45-day usage rights, 2 creative approval rounds. Ready for your sign-off.

close_state.summary: Deal confirmed at ¥65,000 for Bilibili livestream + 3 short videos (highlight + 2 standalone clips), 50/50 payment, 45-day usage rights, 2 creative approval rounds. Ready for your sign-off.

last_message.text: Deal confirmed at ¥65,000 for Bilibili livestream + 3 short videos (highlight + 2 standalone clips), 50/50 payment, 45-day usage rights, 2 creative approval rounds. Ready for your sign-off.

ux_findings: Host Telegram reply exposed debug wording and attached join.json instead of a polished invite handoff.
Guest Telegram reply exposed bridge launch JSON/status instead of a user-facing acknowledgement.
The first product-path prompt created several bogus local room state files before the real room, suggesting the skill prompt still leaks implementation details into model actions.
This run did not validate ASK_OWNER because the guest conceded to the host ceiling.

_redaction_notice.rationale: Thread tokens are redacted. Transcript rows and runtime heartbeats are retained as smoke E2E evidence.

coverage_note: Passed average-user product bootstrap smoke: Telegram clawd create-room path, Telegram invite handoff to Railway Link, remote guest bridge self-launch, turn-taking negotiation, mutual close. Did not cover ASK_OWNER.
